LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-506788, CGC-10-506790 WITH CASE CGC-10-506783 IS GRANTED. THE CASES ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES. CASE 506781 IS THE LEAD CASE AND ALL FURTHER FILINGS WILL BE IN THAT CASE ONLY. BASED ON THE PAPERS SUBMITTED, CONSOLIDATION OF THE CASES APPEARS TO PROMOTE JUDICIAL ECONOMY, ELIMINATES OR MINIMIZES THE RISK OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS, AND WILL NOT PREJUDICE ANY PARTY. TRIAL DATE TO BE SELECTED IN SUCH A WAY THAT THERE WILL ONLY BE ONE DATE FOR ALL THE THREE CASES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) ATTY. HERMAN H. FITZGERALD, TEL. NO. (650) 348-5195 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-506788, CGC-10-506790 WITH CASE CGC-10-506783 is Granted. The cases are consolidated for all purposes. Case 506781 is the lead case and all further filings will be in that case only. Based on the papers submitted, consolidation of the cases appears to promote judicial economy, eliminates or minimizes the risk of inconsistent rulings, and will not prejudice any party. TRIAL DATE TO BE SELECTED IN SUCH A WAY THAT THERE WILL ONLY BE ONE DATE FOR ALL THE THREE CASES. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)
